steps.text: Clear a square of floor in {room} big enough for you and {toy}.
Gather four pillows and two blankets and stack them in your spot.
Stand the pillows on edge to make four fort walls.
Drape one blanket across the top for the roof.
What kind of fort is this for {toy}?
Tuck a book and a flashlight inside. Crawl in with {toy} and read one page out loud.
Cut a peephole between two pillows. Take turns spying out with {toy} and call out three things you see.
Gather two sheets of paper and one crayon. Sit at a flat surface with {toy}.
Fold the first sheet in half the long way, then unfold it.
Fold both top corners down to meet the center crease.
Fold the slanted edges down again to make a sharp nose.
What kind of plane is this?
Fold the wings flat and crisp the creases with your thumbnail.
Bend the back wing tips up a little so the plane wants to climb.
Color a number on each wing — that is your plane's flight number.
Hold the plane behind your ear, point at {toy}, and let it fly. Cheer when it lands!
{toy} wants to play a color hunt. Pick a color to chase first.
Walk slowly around {room} looking for red. Touch each red thing and count out loud.
Walk slowly around {room} looking for blue. Touch each blue thing and count out loud.
Walk slowly around {room} looking for yellow. Touch each yellow thing and count out loud.
Bring {count} of your favorites back and lay them in a line in front of {toy}.
Tell {toy} which one was the easiest to find and which was the trickiest.
Find a tray, plate, or shoebox lid to be your garden frame.
Collect six small pebbles, beads, or buttons and pour them in.
Add one leaf, one twig, or one folded paper triangle as a tree.
Push the pebbles around to make a path winding through the middle.
What lives in this garden with {toy}?
Pile three pebbles into a dragon hill at the path's end. Place {toy} as the dragon's friend.
Make four small pebble nests off the path. Tuck {toy} in as the kindly grandmouse.
Gather {count} small objects from {room} into a messy pile near {toy}.
How should we sort the pile?
Line everything up smallest on the left, biggest on the right.
Line everything up palest on the left, darkest on the right.
Line everything up softest on the left, hardest on the right.
Walk {toy} along the line and announce each thing in order.
Lay paper and three crayons in front of you. {toy} is the art director.
Draw a big round body in the middle of the page.
Add {count} eyes anywhere on the body.
Give the monster four legs — straight, wiggly, or zig-zag, your call.
What does your monster eat?
Draw three purple snacks floating near the monster's mouth.
Draw three perfectly round snacks floating near the monster's mouth.
Whisper the monster's name to {toy}, then say it loud and proud.
Stand in {room} with {toy} and look at the open floor.
What kind of course should we build?
Set two pillows as crawl-under tunnels and one rolled blanket as a jump bar.
Lay a long string or belt on the floor as a wobbly tightrope and place a book at each end.
Walk the course slowly first to remember every station.
Run the course as fast as you can while {toy} cheers from the sidelines.
Take a bow. Tell {toy} the hardest part and the easiest part.
Find a cup, a bowl, or a jar to be your cauldron.
Add three pretend ingredients from {room} — a sock, a paper scrap, a crayon stub.
Stir slowly with a spoon or stick while you whisper the recipe to {toy}.
What does this potion do?
Pretend to drink it, then run a victory lap around {room}.
Pretend to drink it, then crouch behind a {prop} and giggle while {toy} 'looks' for you.
Pretend to pour it on a houseplant, then slowly stand tall with arms out like branches.
Sit on the floor with paper, a crayon, and {toy} the cartographer.
Draw the outline of {room} as a big rectangle on the page.
Add the door, the window, and one piece of furniture as boxes inside.
Pick a hiding spot in {room} and hide a small {prop} there.
Draw a big X on the map at the hiding spot.
How does the treasure hunter find the X?
Draw {count} numbered footprints from the door to the X. Hand the map to a grown-up to follow.
Write one clue word at the top of the map. Hand the map to a grown-up and watch them puzzle it out.
Set up a pretend stovetop on a low table or chair seat with {toy} as the customer.
Gather four pretend ingredients — pebbles, paper balls, leaves, anything.
What is on the menu today?
Drop the ingredients into a pretend pot. Stir slowly and hum a cooking tune.
Press the ingredients flat onto a pretend pan. Flip each one with a big wrist toss.
Slide your dish onto a small plate or paper square in front of {toy}.
Take {toy}'s order review — five stars or four? Bow to the chef!
Find a tall cardboard box, paper-towel tube, or stacked cushions to be your rocket body.
Tape or balance a paper triangle on top as the nose cone.
Add three paper rectangles around the base as fins.
Strap {toy} into the rocket as the brave astronaut.
Count down from ten with one finger raised per number.
Where does the rocket fly today?
Bounce around {room} on tip-toe like low gravity. Plant a paper flag wherever you land.
Crouch low, then stretch up tall like blooming. Tell {toy} which giant flower is friendliest.
Collect paper-towel tubes, books, and a small ball or marble.
Lean two books to make a ramp from a chair down to the floor.
Lay one tube along the ramp so the marble can roll through it.
Where should the marble land at the bottom?
Tape a paper cup at the end of the tube. Aim carefully and roll the marble in.
Place a pillow under the tube's end. Watch the marble bounce-soft and stop.
Drop the marble three times in a row and count how many times it lands cleanly.
Tell {toy} the number out loud and high-five for the best run.
Find a small flat surface — a tray, a windowsill, a low shelf — for the display.
Walk through {room} (or near a window) and gather {count} natural-feeling things: leaves, pebbles, pinecones, feathers.
Lay everything out on the surface in any order.
Pick your favorite find and place it in the very center.
How should we share the display with {toy}?

choices.label: Make a tiny label for each

steps.text: Tear small paper squares and write or draw a label per item. Place each label beside its find.
Pick up each item one at a time and tell {toy} a one-sentence story about where it came from.
Tear paper into four small cards. Sit on the floor with {toy} and a crayon.
Draw a different dance move on each card — a spin, a hop, a wiggle, a freeze.
What kind of music plays while you dance?
Hum or sing fast bouncy tune. Flip a card and do the move.
Hum a slow floaty tune. Flip a card and do the move slooowly.
Clap a steady beat. Flip a card and match the move to your claps.
Flip the rest of the cards one by one until you have danced every move.
Bow to {toy} and stack the cards into a tidy deck for next time.
Bring four picture books and place them open in a half-circle in {room}.
Set one stuffed animal or {toy} inside each open book — those are the habitats.
Walk in a slow loop around the zoo and announce each habitat by name.
Pick the animal that looks hungriest and pretend to feed them three pretend snacks.
What special zoo event happens next?
Carry {toy} on your shoulder and march past every habitat clapping and cheering.
Tip-toe past every habitat whispering goodnight. Tuck {toy} on top of the last book.
Collect six paper cups, plastic cups, or yogurt pots and rinse-clean them if needed.
Set the cups upside-down in a {room} corner. Those are the buildings.
What kind of city is this?
Cluster the cups close together in a tight grid. Add tiny paper-square doors.
Line the cups in two long rows with a 'beach' (a folded blue cloth) between.
Stack cups in a pyramid. Tape paper antennas on top of the highest one.
Lay strips of paper or string between buildings as roads or paths.
Walk {toy} along the streets like a tour guide and name three buildings out loud.
Find a sunny spot near a window in {room} with paper and a crayon.
Stand a small object — a cup, a {prop}, a toy — on the paper so its shadow falls on the page.
Hold the object steady and trace its shadow with the crayon.
Move the object and try a different angle, then trace again.
What should the shadow drawing become?
Add eyes, fangs, and ears inside the shadow outline. Show {toy} the new monster.
Write one tiny letter inside each shadow outline. Hand the page to a grown-up to decode.
What kind of pretend snack are we making with {toy}?
Pretend to drop three colors of fruit into a blender cup. Make whirring sounds with your mouth.
Pretend to stack three layers — bread, jelly, more bread — patting each layer flat.
Give the snack a silly name. Say it three times so {toy} can remember.
Draw the snack on a paper card and write the silly name underneath.
Hand the recipe card to {toy} for the cookbook.
Gather all the stuffed animals you can carry into one pile in {room}.
Sit cross-legged with {toy} on top of the pile as the leader.
How should the animals be stacked?
Stack one animal at a time as high as you dare. Hold your breath at the wobbly part.
Set three on the bottom, two in the middle, one on top. Step back and check the shape.
Pile every animal into a snuggly heap. Wiggle in the middle yourself.
Touch each animal and say its name out loud — invent one for any stranger.
Find a balloon (or a rolled-up sock) for the indoor ball.
Mark two goals — one on each side of {room} — using shoes or pillows.
{toy} drops the ball at center floor. Crouch ready on opposite sides.
What is the rule for moving the ball?
Kick gently — the first goal scored ends the round.
Bat softly — the first goal scored ends the round.
Crawl and blow — the first goal scored ends the round.
